runs.repeatability: STABLE_FAIL for the one failing assertion (reproduced in run-2 below); the other 52 scripts were STABLE_PASS on their single run
notes: Chains 54 scripts with && — chain stopped after test:clinic-bulk-export failed, so test:security-incidents (the 54th/last script) never ran in this invocation.
2481 = sum of all passed counts across the 53 scripts that ran (see per-script breakdown in scripts[] below).

The single failure is clinicBulkExport.test.ts "status DTO never serializes sensitive fields" — root-caused to a Windows/CRLF line-ending artifact (git core.autocrlf=true checks the file out with \r\n; the test does an exact-string indexOf() search containing a literal \n), NOT a real product defect. Confirmed via byte-level inspection of server/src/routes/clinicBulkExport.ts.

runs.command: 6 individual npm run test:* invocations (consent-resume, meta-template, outbound, no-show-follow-up-parity, overdue-installments, overdue-receivables)
runs.repeatability: overdue-installments STABLE_FAIL (reproduced 2x, identical 2 failures both times); other 5 STABLE_PASS on single run
notes: These 6 scripts exist in server/package.json but are NEVER called from the `npm run test` chain — they were run individually to complete the inventory.

test:overdue-installments has 2 GENUINE deterministic failures (not environmental, not CRLF-related): the test asserts the production `overdueInstallmentWhere()` query filters status strictly to the literal value "pending" and that isInstallmentOverdue() treats a literal status="overdue" value as never-written/always-false. The actual production code now queries `status: { in: ['pending', 'overdue'] }` and isInstallmentOverdue() returns true for status="overdue". This is real behavioral drift between an orphaned test (never run by CI or the full `npm run test` chain) and the production code it was written to protect.
Re-run for the 2026-07-19 rebaseline — all 6 scripts (101 passed, 2 failed total) reproduced identically, including the same 2 named test:overdue-installments failures.

blockedMeasurements.reason: Requires a reachable PostgreSQL instance via DATABASE_URL. No committed docker-compose.yml or equivalent disposable-DB provisioning exists in the repository. Docker CLI (29.6.1) is present on this machine but was deliberately not used to improvise a database, per this task’s explicit instruction to classify as BLOCKED rather than invent setup when no committed convention exists.
blockedMeasurements.exactMissingPrerequisite: A committed, repository-documented disposable PostgreSQL provisioning method (e.g. docker-compose.yml + seed instructions), OR explicit user-provided DATABASE_URL to an already-running disposable instance.

blockedMeasurements.reason: Same as above — explicitly documented in each script’s own header as requiring `DATABASE_URL=postgresql://.../throwaway_db` and a prior `prisma migrate deploy`, and explicitly NOT wired into npm test by original design.
Same as securityIncident.test.ts — requires a reachable PostgreSQL instance via DATABASE_URL; no committed disposable-DB provisioning exists in the repository.

warnings: This is a Windows checkout with git core.autocrlf=true — at least one test (clinicBulkExport.test.ts) is demonstrably sensitive to this and would need re-verification on a Linux/CI runner (its own comments state some of its sub-cases are "skipped on win32 ... verified for real in the Linux container run", confirming the test author already knew about Windows/Linux behavioral differences for OTHER cases in the same file).

Pass/fail counts for scripts that chain multiple .test.ts files (test:auth, test:instagram, test:meta-wa) are reported at the script level in this file; per-file breakdowns for those specific files are recorded in F0-005_test_inventory.json testFiles[].

Only ONE full-suite run of `npm run test` was performed, per this task’s 2-run cap for full suites; only the single failing suite (test:clinic-bulk-export) and the orphan test:overdue-installments were repeated a 2nd time to confirm stability.

No coverage percentage tool (nyc/c8/istanbul) exists in either package.json — no line/branch coverage numbers are reported anywhere in this task’s output, consistent with the task’s prohibition on claiming coverage percentages without an actual tool.
